title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's lawyers amended a court filing to clarify that an unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore's unborn child.
The amendment followed a report that mistakenly named Farwell’s brother as the father, which was attributed to a misunderstanding.
Farwell, a former Stoughton officer, faces charges of killing Birchmore in 2021 and seeks release on $50,000 bail.

title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lily Penzone, 21, from Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her best friend Abby's late father.
Abby's father, a former Boston Marathoner, passed away in a trauma accident; Penzone runs to preserve his legacy.
Penzone is raising funds for Brigham and Women's Stepping Strong team to support trauma research and awareness.

title: Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston College senior Merideth Greene is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Last Call Foundation.
Greene aims to honor first responders, especially firefighters, influenced by the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing.
The Last Call Foundation advocates for firefighter health, focusing on cancer screenings and safer protective gear.

title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Peach Farm will close temporarily as current owners retire after 31 years, with new ownership planned.
The Boston Licensing Board approved the transfer of Peach Farm’s food and beverage licenses to new owners.
New manager Washan Ching plans to retain the restaurant's name and concept after reopening.

title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts U.S. Attorney Leah Foley announced a task force to tackle benefit and voter fraud, with a public hotline for reports.
Assistant U.S. Attorneys Philip Mallard and Mark Grady will lead the team, focusing on SNAP, MassHealth, and election-related offenses.
The ACLU of Massachusetts criticized the task force's voter fraud focus, arguing it's a non-issue and undermines election integrity.

title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces Relax Row, a couch-style seating option for economy class, launching in 2027.
Relax Row features three seats that transform into a lie-flat space, ideal for couples and families on long-haul flights.
The seating will be available on over 200 Boeing 787 and 777 aircraft by 2030, with pricing yet to be announced.

title: Ad Club’s Women’s Leadership Forum stays alive amid DEI pushback
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Ad Club's annual Women's Leadership Forum at Gillette Stadium addressed workplace challenges and celebrated connections among over 1,000 women.
CEO Barb Reilly emphasized the forum's importance amid DEI program reassessment post-Trump executive order impact on corporate climates.
Speakers like Nadia Murad highlighted resilience, inspiring attendees to embrace unexpected opportunities and self-transformation in their careers.

title: Juvenile injured in Allston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A juvenile was injured in a shooting near 222 Brighton Ave in Allston Thursday at 6:45 p.m.
The victim suffered a non-life-threatening gunshot wound and was treated at a local hospital.
Boston police are investigating; no arrests have been made, and the shooter remains at large.

title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone, 57, is running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital.
Iannacone's participation supports Jace Perkins, a former NICU baby now thriving at nearly 12 years old.
Jace's survival is credited to the Boston Children's NICU and neurology team led by Dr. Benjamin Warf.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent trends in Greater Boston news.
Peter Chianca is the General Assignment Editor at Boston.com, serving since 2019.
The quiz covers various stories relevant to Greater Boston, aiming to keep locals informed.
